steps: With 11 values, the median is the 6th value when sorted. Position = \frac{11+1}{2} = 6.
Data set A sorted: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, 8, 11, 11, 14, 14, 14. The 6th value is 8, so the median of A is 8.
Data set B sorted: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 11, 11, 14. The 6th value is 8, so the median of B is 8.
Both medians equal 8, so they are equal. That matches choice C — C is correct.

choices.text: The median of data set A is greater than the median of data set B.
choices.explanation: • Median of A = 8 and median of B = 8. Equal, not greater. Eliminate A.

choices.text: The median of data set A is less than the median of data set B.
choices.explanation: • Median of A = 8 and median of B = 8. Equal, not less. Eliminate B.

choices.text: The medians of data sets A and B are equal.
choices.explanation: • The 6th value in each sorted set is 8. Both medians equal 8. That matches choice C — C is correct.

choices.text: There is not enough information to compare the medians.
choices.explanation: • All values are given explicitly, so there is sufficient information. Eliminate D.
